content.problemStatement:
Harsh was recently gifted a book consisting of NNN pages. Each page contains exactly MMM words printed on it. As he was bored, he decided to count the number of words in the book.

Help Harsh find the total number of words in the book.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two space-separated integers on a single line, NNN and MMM — the number of pages and the number of words on each page, respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the total number of words in the book.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and M — the number of pages in the book and the number of words on each page.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the total number of words in the book (N × M).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: 1 page × 1 word = 1.
Test case 2: 4 pages × 2 words = 8.
Test case 3: 2 pages × 4 words = 8.
Test case 4: 95 pages × 42 words = 3990.
First test case: 10 × 20 = 200. Second test case: 7 × 9 = 63.

solution.approach: For each test case we just need to multiply the two given integers. The product fits easily in 64‑bit signed integer because the maximum possible value is 100 × 100 = 10 000. Iterate over all test cases, compute N*M and print the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time multiplication per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of input size.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    idx = 1
    out = []
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(data[idx]); m = int(data[idx + 1]); idx += 2
        out.append(str(n * m))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
